name: add_prefix_suffix
description: Add prefix/suffix to parameter values. Preview with dryRun=true.

parameterName.description: Name of the parameter to modify.

prefix.description: Prefix to prepend to existing value.

suffix.description: Suffix to append to existing value.

separator.description: Separator between prefix/suffix and existing value. Default: empty string.

scope.description: Scope: whole_model, active_view, or selection. Default: whole_model.

skipEmpty.description: Skip elements with empty parameter value. Default: true.

filterValue.description: Only modify elements whose parameter value contains this text.

dryRun.description: If true (default), preview changes without applying. Set to false to execute.

name: add_shared_parameter
description: Add a shared parameter from the shared parameter file to categories.

parameterName.description: Name of the shared parameter to add.

groupName.description: Group name in the shared parameter file.

isInstance.description: If true, binds as an instance parameter.

parameterGroup.description: Display group for the parameter in the element properties panel.

name: ai_element_filter
description:
An intelligent Revit element querying tool designed specifically for AI assistants to retrieve detailed element information from Revit projects. This tool allows the AI to request elements matching specific criteria (such as category, type, visibility, or spatial location) and then perform further analysis on the returned data to answer complex user queries about Revit model elements. Example: When a user asks 'Find all walls taller than 5m in the project', the AI would: 1) Call this tool with parameters: {"filterCategory": "OST_Walls", "includeInstances": true}, 2) Receive detailed information about all wall instances in the project, 3) Process the returned data to filter walls with height > 5000mm, 4) Present the filtered results to the user with relevant details.

GUIDANCE:
- Find all doors on Level 1: category="Doors", parameterFilters=[{name:"Level", value:"Level 1"}]
- Find walls by type: category="Walls", parameterFilters=[{name:"Type", value:"Generic - 200mm"}]
- Find rooms by department: category="Rooms", parameterFilters=[{name:"Department", value:"Office"}]

TIPS:
- Most powerful query tool — supports multi-parameter filtering
- Returns element IDs for use with other tools (modify, delete, set_parameters)
- Use export_elements_data for tabular data extraction instead
- Combine with operate_element to select/highlight filtered results

filterCategory.description: Enumeration of built-in element categories in Revit used for filtering and identifying specific element types (e.g., OST_Walls, OST_Floors, OST_GenericModel). Note that furniture elements may be classified as either OST_Furniture or OST_GenericModel categories, requiring flexible selection approaches

filterElementType.description: The Revit element type name used for filtering specific elements by their class or type (e.g., 'Wall', 'Floor', 'Autodesk.Revit.DB.Wall'). Gets or sets the name of the Revit element type to be filtered.

filterFamilySymbolId.description: The ElementId of a specific FamilySymbol (type) in Revit used for filtering elements by their type (e.g., '123456', '789012'). Gets or sets the ElementId of the FamilySymbol to be used as a filter criterion. Use '-1' if no specific FamilySymbol filtering is needed.

includeTypes.description: Determines whether to include element types (such as wall types, door types, etc.) in the selection results. When set to true, element types will be included; when false, they will be excluded.

includeInstances.description: Determines whether to include element instances (such as placed walls, doors, etc.) in the selection results. When set to true, element instances will be included; when false, they will be excluded.

filterVisibleInCurrentView.description: Determines whether to only return elements that are visible in the current view. When set to true, only elements visible in the current view will be returned. Note: This filter only applies to element instances, not type elements.

boundingBoxMin.description: The minimum point coordinates (in mm) for spatial bounding box filtering. When set along with boundingBoxMax, only elements that intersect with this bounding box will be returned. Set to null to disable this filter.

boundingBoxMax.description: The maximum point coordinates (in mm) for spatial bounding box filtering. When set along with boundingBoxMin, only elements that intersect with this bounding box will be returned. Set to null to disable this filter.

maxElements.description: The maximum number of elements to find in a single tool invocation. Default is 50. Values exceeding 50 are not recommended for performance reasons.

data.description: Configuration parameters for the Revit element filter tool. These settings determine which elements will be selected from the Revit project based on various filtering criteria. Multiple filters can be combined to achieve precise element selection. All spatial coordinates should be provided in millimeters.

compact.description: Return summary counts only, without full data arrays. Saves tokens for large results.

name: align_viewports
description: Align viewports across sheets by placement or model coordinates.

sourceViewportId.description: ID of the reference viewport to align to.

name: analyze_model_statistics
description:
Analyze model complexity with element counts. Returns detailed statistics about the Revit model including total element counts, total types, total families, views, sheets, counts by category (with type/family breakdown), and level-by-level element distribution. Useful for model auditing, performance analysis, and understanding model composition.

GUIDANCE:
- Model overview: element counts by category, type, family, and level
- Quality check: identify categories with unexpected element counts
- Pre-audit: run before check_model_health for a quick complexity assessment

TIPS:
- Useful for understanding model scope before detailed operations
- Compare counts across levels to check consistency
- Large element counts may indicate modeling issues

includeDetailedTypes.description: Whether to include detailed breakdown by family and type within each category. Defaults to true.

compact.description: Return summary counts only, without full data arrays. Saves tokens for large results.

name: apply_view_template
description: Apply or remove a view template on one or more views.

action.description: Action: list templates, apply to views, or remove from views (default: list)

viewIds.description: View IDs to apply/remove template (required for apply/remove)

templateName.description: Template name to search for (used if templateId not provided)

name: audit_families
description:
Comprehensive family audit: health score, unused families, in-place families, instance counts per family/type, category breakdown, and cleanup recommendations.

GUIDANCE:
- Full audit: call with no parameters for comprehensive family analysis
- Filter by category: categoryFilter="Doors" to audit only specific category
- Include unused: includeUnused=true (default) shows families with zero instances

TIPS:
- Health score factors: unused families, in-place families, CAD imports
- Recommendations include specific cleanup actions
- Use purge_unused to remove families flagged as unused
- Category breakdown shows which areas need most attention

includeUnused.description: Include unused families in results. Default: true.

categoryFilter.description: Filter families by category name (e.g. 'Doors'). If omitted, audits all categories.

name: batch_create_sheets
description:
Create multiple sheets at once with title blocks and optional view placement. Each sheet can have its own number, name, title block, and views to place.

GUIDANCE:
- Create multiple sheets: provide array of {sheetNumber, sheetName, titleBlockName}
- Auto-sequence: use a numbering pattern like A101, A102, A103
- With views: optionally specify viewIds to auto-place on each sheet

TIPS:
- Sheet numbers must be unique — check existing sheets first
- Use get_available_family_types with "Title Blocks" to find title block names
- Use place_viewport after creation to position views on sheets
- Use align_viewports to align view positions across multiple sheets

titleBlockName.description: Title block family type name for this sheet

viewIds.description: View IDs to place on this sheet

sheets.description: Array of sheet definitions to create

defaultTitleBlockName.description: Default title block for sheets that don't specify one

name: batch_export
description: Export multiple views/sheets to PDF, DWG, DXF, DGN, or image formats.

sheetIds.description: Sheet IDs to export (for PDF/DWG). If empty, exports all sheets.

exportPath.description: Directory path for exported files. If omitted, uses temp folder.

paperSize.description: Paper size for PDF export (default: A4)

name: batch_modify_view_range
description: Batch-modify view range (cut plane, top, bottom) for plan views.

viewIds.description: IDs of plan views to modify.

topOffsetMm.description: Top clip plane offset from level in mm.

cutPlaneOffsetMm.description: Cut plane offset from level in mm (typically 1200mm).

bottomOffsetMm.description: Bottom clip plane offset from level in mm.

viewDepthOffsetMm.description: View depth plane offset from level in mm.

name: batch_rename
description: Batch rename elements using find/replace, prefix, suffix, or regex.

targetCategory.description: Category of elements to rename (used when elementIds is not provided)

findText.description: Text to find in existing names (for find/replace mode)

dryRun.description: If true (default), only previews changes without applying.

name: bulk_modify_parameter_values
description: Bulk set, prefix, suffix, find/replace, or clear parameter values.

elementIds.description: Element IDs to modify. If omitted, uses categoryName to find elements.

categoryName.description: Revit category name (e.g. 'Walls', 'Doors'). Used when elementIds not provided.

parameterName.description: Name of the parameter to modify.

operation.description: set, prefix, suffix, find_replace, or clear.

value.description: Value to set/prefix/suffix. Required for set, prefix, suffix operations.

onlyEmpty.description: If true, only modify elements where parameter is currently empty.

dryRun.description: If true (default), preview changes without applying. Set to false to execute.

name: cad_link_cleanup
description: Analyze and clean up imported/linked CAD files in the model.

action.description: Action: list all CAD imports/links, or delete them (default: list)

deleteImports.description: Delete CAD imports (embedded files) when action is delete

deleteLinks.description: Delete CAD links (referenced files) when action is delete

elementIds.description: Specific CAD element IDs to delete (overrides deleteImports/deleteLinks flags)

name: calculate_rai
description: Calculate Rapporto Aeroilluminante (RAI) — window-to-floor area ratio per Italian building code DM 5/7/1975. Checks compliance against minimum threshold (default 1/8 = 0.125). Returns per-room breakdown with window details, areas in m², and compliance status.

roomIds.description: Room ElementIds to analyze. Omit for all rooms.

roomNumbers.description: Room numbers to query (alternative to roomIds).

levelName.description: Filter rooms by level name (partial match).

minRatio.description: Minimum acceptable ratio (default 1/8 = 0.125 per DM 5/7/1975). Use 0.1 for offices (1/10).

includeServiceRooms.description: Include rooms typically exempt from RAI (bagni, corridoi, ripostigli). Default false.

phaseName.description: Phase name for window-room association (partial match). Default: active view phase, then last project phase.

ratioOverrides.description: Per-room-type ratio overrides. Key = keyword in room name, value = minimum ratio. Example: {"cucina": 0.125, "ufficio": 0.1, "camera": 0.125}. First keyword match wins; unmatched rooms use minRatio.

name: change_element_type
description: Change the family type of one or more elements.

elementIds.description: Element IDs to change type for

targetTypeId.description: Target type element ID to change to

targetTypeName.description: Target type name to search for (used if targetTypeId not provided)

targetFamilyName.description: Target family name to narrow type search

name: check_family_health
description: Analyze loaded families for file size, import issues, and naming.

includeFileSize.description: Measure family file sizes by temporarily opening each family document.

sortBy.description: Sort results by: name, size, or instance_count. Default: size.

name: check_model_health
description:
Comprehensive BIM model health audit. Returns a health score (0-100), grade (A-F), and detailed breakdown: warnings count and top 10 types, in-place families, imported CAD, unplaced rooms, unused views, detail lines. Includes actionable recommendations. Use this to assess model quality before delivery or identify cleanup priorities.

GUIDANCE:
- Full audit: call with no parameters for comprehensive model health check
- Returns health score (0-100), grade (A-F), and specific recommendations
- Run periodically during model development for quality assurance

TIPS:
- Score includes: warnings count, unused families, CAD imports, in-place families
- Use audit_families for detailed family-level health analysis
- Use get_warnings for the full list of Revit warnings
- Address high-impact issues (score deductions > 10 points) first

name: clash_detection
description:
Detect geometric intersections (clashes) between two sets of elements. Specify by category names (e.g., 'Ducts' vs 'StructuralFraming') or specific element IDs. Returns pairs of clashing elements.

GUIDANCE:
- Check wall-pipe clashes: category1="Walls", category2="Pipes"
- MEP coordination: category1="Ducts", category2="Structural Framing"
- Full model check: provide two categories to check for intersections

TIPS:
- Returns pairs of clashing element IDs with intersection details
- Use operate_element to highlight/isolate clashing elements
- Focus on critical clashes (structural vs MEP) first
- Combine with section_box_from_selection to zoom into clash locations

categoryA.description: Category name for set A (e.g., Walls, Ducts, Pipes, StructuralFraming, Columns, Floors)

elementIdsA.description: Specific element IDs for set A (overrides categoryA)

elementIdsB.description: Specific element IDs for set B (overrides categoryB)

maxResults.description: Maximum number of clashes to return (default: 100)

name: clear_parameter_values
description: Clear parameter values on elements by category, view, or selection.

filterValue.description: Only clear elements whose parameter contains this value.

parameterType.description: Whether to clear instance or type parameter.

dryRun.description: If true (default), preview changes without applying. Set to false to execute.

name: color_elements
description: Colorize elements by parameter value with auto or custom colors.

categoryName.description: The name of the Revit category to color (e.g., 'Walls', 'Doors', 'Rooms')

parameterName.description: The name of the parameter to use for grouping and coloring elements

useGradient.description: Whether to use a gradient color scheme instead of random colors

customColors.description: Optional array of custom RGB colors to use for specific parameter values

name: copy_elements
description: Copy elements with translation offset, optionally to another level.

elementIds.description: Array of element IDs to copy

sourceViewId.description: Source view ID where elements currently exist

targetViewId.description: Target view ID to copy elements to

offsetX.description: X offset in mm for the copied elements (default: 0)

offsetY.description: Y offset in mm for the copied elements (default: 0)

offsetZ.description: Z offset in mm for the copied elements (default: 0)

name: create_array
description: Create linear or radial arrays of elements.

count.description: Number of copies to create (total = original + count)

spacingX.description: X spacing in mm between copies (for linear array, default: 0)

spacingY.description: Y spacing in mm between copies (for linear array, default: 0)

spacingZ.description: Z spacing in mm between copies (for linear array, default: 0)

centerX.description: X center of rotation in mm (for radial array)

centerY.description: Y center of rotation in mm (for radial array)

totalAngle.description: Total angle in degrees for radial array (default: 360)

name: create_callout_from_rooms
description: Create callout views from rooms with auto-naming and crop regions.

roomIds.description: Specific room element IDs. If empty, uses levelName or all rooms.

levelName.description: Level name to filter rooms (e.g. 'Level 1'). Ignored if roomIds provided.

offset.description: Boundary offset around room in mm (default: 300mm).

viewTemplateId.description: View template element ID to apply to created callouts.

name: create_color_legend
description: Color-code elements by parameter and place a color legend on a sheet.

parameterName.description: Parameter to group and colorize by (e.g. 'Department', 'Area').

categories.description: Categories to include (e.g. Rooms, Walls, Doors). Default: Rooms.

createLegendView.description: Create a Legend view showing color swatches and values. Default: true.

legendTitle.description: Title text for the legend view. Default: 'Color Legend'.

targetViewId.description: Element ID of the view to apply overrides in. If omitted, uses the active view.

name: create_dimensions
description: Create dimension lines between references or elements.

startPoint.description: Start point of the dimension line (mm)

endPoint.description: End point of the dimension line (mm)

linePoint.description: Location of the dimension line itself (mm).

dimensionStyleId.description: Element ID of the dimension style to apply. -1 for default style

viewId.description: Element ID of the view to create the dimension in. -1 for active view

name: create_door_schedule_by_room
description: Create a Door Schedule organized by room with family, type, and dimensions

includeToRoom.description: Include To Room field. Default: true

name: create_elevations_from_rooms
description: Create interior elevation views for rooms (N/S/E/W).

roomIds.description: Specific room IDs to create elevations for.

viewType.description: Type of view to create. Default: elevation.

directions.description: Which directions to create elevations for. Default: all four directions.

offset.description: Offset from room boundary in mm. Default: 300.

viewTemplateId.description: Element ID of view template to apply.

name: create_filled_region
description: Create a filled region in a view from boundary points.

boundaryPoints.description: Boundary points in mm defining the filled region outline (minimum 3 points)

viewId.description: View ID to create the region in (default: active view)

filledRegionTypeName.description: Filled region type name (default: first available)

name: create_floor
description: Create a floor element from boundary points on a level.

boundaryPoints.description: Array of 2D points defining the floor boundary in mm (minimum 3 points).

roomId.description: Room element ID to derive floor boundary from (alternative to boundaryPoints)

floorTypeName.description: Floor type name to use. If omitted, uses the first available floor type.

levelElevation.description: Level elevation in mm for the floor.

isStructural.description: Whether the floor is structural (default: false)

name: create_grid
description: Create grid lines (linear or arc) with optional naming pattern.

xCount.description: Number of grid lines along X-axis (vertical grids)

xSpacing.description: Spacing between X-axis grid lines in millimeters

xStartLabel.description: Starting label for X-axis grids (e.g., 'A' or '1')

yCount.description: Number of grid lines along Y-axis (horizontal grids)

ySpacing.description: Spacing between Y-axis grid lines in millimeters

yStartLabel.description: Starting label for Y-axis grids (e.g., '1' or 'A')

xExtentMin.description: Minimum extent along X-axis in mm (where Y-axis grids start)

xExtentMax.description: Maximum extent along X-axis in mm (where Y-axis grids end)

yExtentMin.description: Minimum extent along Y-axis in mm (where X-axis grids start)

yExtentMax.description: Maximum extent along Y-axis in mm (where X-axis grids end)

elevation.description: Elevation for grid lines in mm (Z-coordinate)

xStartPosition.description: Starting position for first X-axis grid in mm

yStartPosition.description: Starting position for first Y-axis grid in mm

name: create_level
description: Create new levels at specified elevations.

elevation.description: Elevation of the level in millimeters (mm) from project origin

isMainLevel.description: Whether this is a main level (default: true)

isBuildingStory.description: Whether this level represents a building story (default: true)

viewPlanOffset.description: Optional view plan offset in mm

viewSectionOffset.description: Optional view section offset in mm

viewElevationOffset.description: Optional view elevation offset in mm

createFloorPlan.description: Whether to create a floor plan view for this level (default: true)

createCeilingPlan.description: Whether to create a ceiling plan view for this level (default: true)

name: create_line_based_element
description: Create line-based elements (walls, beams, pipes, ducts, etc.).

category.description: Revit built-in category (e.g., OST_Walls, OST_StructuralFraming, OST_DuctCurves)

typeId.description: The ID of the family type to create.

locationLine.description: The line defining the element's location

thickness.description: Thickness/width of the element (e.g., wall thickness)

height.description: Height of the element (e.g., wall height)

data.description: Array of line-based elements to create

name: create_material
description: Create a new material or duplicate an existing one. Optionally set color, transparency, and class.

duplicateFrom.description: Name of existing material to duplicate. If omitted, creates a blank material.

name: create_material_takeoff_schedule
description: Create a Material Takeoff schedule with quantities by category

name: create_placeholder_sheets
description: Create multiple placeholder sheets with sequential numbering.

titleBlockId.description: Title block type element ID (for 'convert' action)

name: create_point_based_element
description: Place point-based family instances at coordinates.

name.description: Description of the element (e.g., door, window)

typeId.description: The ID of the family type to create.

locationPoint.description: The position coordinates where the element will be placed

width.description: Width of the element in mm

depth.description: Depth of the element in mm

height.description: Height of the element in mm

hostWallId.description: The ElementId of a specific wall to use as host for doors/windows. If not provided, the nearest wall will be auto-detected.

facingFlipped.description: Whether to flip the facing direction of the door/window. When true, the element faces the opposite side of the wall.

data.description: Array of point-based elements to create

name: create_revision
description: List, create, or assign revisions to sheets.

sheetIds.description: Sheet IDs to add revision to (for add_to_sheets)

name: create_room
description: Create rooms at specified locations with optional parameters.

x.description: X coordinate in mm (should be inside enclosed walls)

y.description: Y coordinate in mm (should be inside enclosed walls)

z.description: Z coordinate in mm (typically 0 or level elevation)

location.description: Location point in mm - must be inside enclosed walls.

upperLimitId.description: Upper limit Level ElementId for room height

limitOffset.description: Offset from upper limit in mm

baseOffset.description: Offset from base level in mm

name: create_room_finish_schedule
description: Create a Room Finish Schedule with Number, Name, Level, Area, and finish fields

name: create_schedule
description: Create native Revit schedule with all types (regular, key, material takeoff, sheet/view list, etc.), presets, fields, filters, sorting, grouping, and display options.

preset.description: Preset template that auto-configures fields, filters, sorting, and grouping for common schedule types

familyId.description: Family ElementId required for note_block schedule type

parameterName.description: Parameter name to add as a schedule field

fieldType.description: Field type (Instance, Type, Count, Formula, Phasing)

gridColumnWidth.description: Column width in the schedule grid (in sheet units)

fields.description: Fields to include in the schedule

filterType.description: Equal, NotEqual, GreaterThan, LessThan, Contains, etc.

filters.description: Filters to apply to the schedule

sortOrder.description: Sort order within the group. Default: Ascending

showHeader.description: Show group header row. Default: true

showFooter.description: Show group footer row with totals. Default: false

showBlankLine.description: Show blank line between groups. Default: false

groupFields.description: Group fields to organize schedule rows into collapsible groups

isItemized.description: If true, show every instance as a separate row; if false, collapse identical rows. Default: true

showGrandTotal.description: Show grand total row at bottom of schedule. Default: false

showGrandTotalCount.description: Show count in the grand total row. Default: false

grandTotalTitle.description: Custom title for the grand total row (e.g. 'Grand Total')

includeLinkedFiles.description: Include elements from linked Revit files. Default: false

name: create_sheet
description: Create a new sheet with a title block.

titleBlockFamilyName.description: Title block family name. If not specified, uses the first available title block

titleBlockTypeName.description: Title block type name within the family

titleBlockTypeId.description: Title block type ID (alternative to family/type name)

name: create_sheet_list_schedule
description: Create a Sheet List schedule with sheet numbers, names, and revision info

name: create_structural_framing_system
description: Create a beam system from boundary lines on a level.

xMin.description: Minimum X coordinate of the rectangular boundary in millimeters

xMax.description: Maximum X coordinate of the rectangular boundary in millimeters

yMin.description: Minimum Y coordinate of the rectangular boundary in millimeters

yMax.description: Maximum Y coordinate of the rectangular boundary in millimeters

directionEdge.description: Which edge defines the beam direction.

layoutRule.description: Layout rule type. Currently only 'fixed_distance' is supported.

elevation.description: Elevation offset from the level in millimeters.

is3d.description: Whether to create a 3D beam system.

name: create_surface_based_element
description: Create surface-based elements (floors, ceilings, roofs) from points.

name.description: Description of the element (e.g., floor, ceiling)

category.description: The Revit built-in category for the element.

typeId.description: The ID of the family type to create.

outerLoop.description: Array of line segments defining the boundary

data.description: Array of surface-based elements to create

name: create_text_note
description: Create a text note in a view at specified coordinates.

position.description: Position of the text note in mm

viewId.description: View ID to place the text note in (default: active view)

textNoteTypeId.description: Text note type ID (default: first available type)

width.description: Text note width in mm (0 = auto width). Controls text wrapping

textNotes.description: Array of text notes to create

name: create_type_container
description: Create a dedicated level ('container') far below the model, place one sample of each basic wall type on it, cut a section across the row, and tag every sample. A documentation aid that pairs with generate_type_schedules. Dimensions in mm.

sampleLengthMm.description: Horizontal run of each sample stub (along Y). Default 500.

sampleHeightMm.description: Height of each sample, from the container level. Default 1000.

gapMm.description: Clear gap between consecutive samples (along X). Default 500.

levelDropMm.description: How far below the lowest existing level to place the container level. Default 10000.

levelName.description: Name of the container level. Default 'container'.

tagTypeName.description: Wall tag type/family to use (matched by type name, family name, or 'Family : Type'). Default: first available wall tag.

sectionName.description: Legacy — one section per wall type is now created, named after the type. Kept for back-compat.

name: create_view
description: Create floor plan, ceiling plan, section, elevation, or 3D views.

levelElevation.description: Level elevation in mm (for plan views, or origin Z for sections)

viewFamilyTypeName.description: Specific view family type name to use

name: create_view_filter
description: Create, apply, or list view filters with parameter-based rules.

filterName.description: Name for the new filter or name of existing filter to apply

categoryNames.description: Category names to filter (e.g. ['Walls', 'Floors']). Required for 'create'.

viewId.description: View ID to apply the filter to (for 'apply', default: active view)

isVisible.description: Whether filtered elements are visible (default: true)

name: create_view_list_schedule
description: Create a View List schedule with view names, types, scales, and sheet placement

name: create_view_template
description: Create a new view template from an existing view. Copies all view settings (scale, detail level, visibility, overrides).

templateName.description: Name for the new view template.

sourceViewId.description: Source view ID to create template from. Default: active view.

sourceViewName.description: Source view name (partial match). Alternative to sourceViewId.

name: create_views_from_rooms
description:
Automatically create Callout, Section, or Elevation views from rooms. Each room gets views fitted to its boundary with configurable offset. Supports custom naming patterns with {RoomNumber}, {RoomName}, {Level} placeholders.

GUIDANCE:
- Callout views: viewType="callout" — creates cropped plan views per room
- Section views: viewType="section" — creates sections through each room
- Elevation views: viewType="elevation" — creates 4 interior elevations per room
- All rooms: set allRooms=true or provide specific roomIds

TIPS:
- Use export_room_data first to see available rooms and their IDs
- Naming pattern supports: {RoomNumber}, {RoomName}, {Level} placeholders
- Apply view templates after creation with apply_view_template
- Created views can be placed on sheets with place_viewport

roomIds.description: Room IDs to create views for. If omitted, processes all placed rooms.

allRooms.description: Process all rooms. Default: true when no roomIds specified.

viewType.description: Type of view to create. 'elevation' creates 4 views (N/E/S/W). Default: callout.

offsetMm.description: Offset buffer around room boundary in mm. Default: 500.

viewTemplateName.description: Name of view template to apply.

name: create_window_schedule_by_room
description: Create a Window Schedule organized by room with family, type, dimensions, and sill/head heights

name: create_workset
description: Create a new user workset in the current workshared project.

name.description: Name for the new workset. Cannot contain { } [ ] | ; characters.

name: delete_element
description: Delete elements by ID with optional dryRun preview.

elementIds.description: The IDs of the elements to delete

dryRun.description: If true (default), preview which elements would be deleted without actually deleting them

name: delete_schedule
description: Delete a Revit schedule by ID or name. Requires confirm=true to proceed.

scheduleId.description: Schedule ElementId. Provide either scheduleId or scheduleName.

confirm.description: Must be true to confirm deletion. Safety check to prevent accidental deletions.

name: delete_selection
description: Delete currently selected elements with optional dryRun preview.

name.description: Name of the saved selection to delete

name: delete_workset
description: Delete a user workset and move its elements to another workset. Requires user confirmation.

worksetName.description: Name of the workset to delete

moveToWorksetName.description: Name of the workset where elements will be moved before deletion

name: duplicate_schedule
description: Duplicate a Revit schedule by ID or name with a new name.

scheduleId.description: Schedule ElementId. Provide either scheduleId or scheduleName.

name: duplicate_sheet_with_content
description: Duplicate a sheet including all annotations and detail items.

sheetId.description: ID of the source sheet to duplicate.

copies.description: Number of copies to create. Default: 1.

duplicateViews.description: Duplicate placed views with detailing. Default: true.

keepLegends.description: Place legends on new sheets. Default: true.

keepSchedules.description: Place schedules on new sheets. Default: true.

name: duplicate_sheet_with_views
description: Duplicate a sheet and its placed views onto a new sheet.

sheetId.description: ElementId of the source sheet to duplicate.

copies.description: Number of copies to create. Default: 1.

duplicateViews.description: Duplicate placed views (not just reference them). Default: true.

keepLegends.description: Keep legend views on new sheets. Default: true.

keepSchedules.description: Keep schedules on new sheets. Default: true.

name: duplicate_view
description: Duplicate a view (independent, dependent, or with detailing).

newNamePrefix.description: Prefix for the new view name

newNameSuffix.description: Suffix for the new view name

name: export_elements_data
description:
Export elements by category with selected parameters. Returns columns + rows in JSON or CSV.
Default limit is 100 elements. Response includes truncated:true and totalCount when results are limited.

TOKEN OPTIMIZATION: Always specify parameterNames to return only needed columns — this skips expensive parameter discovery and dramatically reduces response size.

GUIDANCE: Core data-extraction workflow:
- Export all doors with Mark, Level, Width, Height:
    categories=["Doors"], parameterNames=["Mark","Level","Width","Height"]
- Get all walls including type info:
    categories=["Walls"], includeTypeParameters=true
- Filter rooms with area > 20 m²:
    categories=["Rooms"], filterParameterName="Area", filterValue="20", filterOperator="greater_than"
- Export ALL parameters of every element in a category:
    categories=["Furniture"], parameterNames=[] (omit for all params)
- Get a CSV ready for Excel editing:
    outputFormat="csv"

QUICK PROMPTS:
- "export all [category] data"          → use that category, omit parameterNames for all params
- "compare [category] type parameters"  → includeTypeParameters=true
- "find elements where [param]=[value]" → filterParameterName + filterValue + filterOperator
- "edit parameters in bulk"             → export first, then pass rows to sync_csv_parameters

categories.description: Category names to export (e.g. 'Walls', 'Doors', 'Rooms', 'Furniture'). Leave empty to export all model categories (may be slow on large models).

parameterNames.description: Parameter names to include as columns (e.g. 'Mark', 'Level', 'Comments'). When provided, skips expensive parameter discovery — faster and smaller response. Leave empty to export ALL parameters discovered on the elements.

includeTypeParameters.description: When true, also collect parameters from the element's Family Type. Useful for comparing type-level data such as Width, Height, or Material.

includeElementId.description: Include an 'ElementId' column in the output. Required if you intend to pass the result to sync_csv_parameters.

outputFormat.description: Output format. 'json' returns an array of objects; 'csv' returns a semicolon-delimited string.

maxElements.description: Maximum elements to return. Default 100. Use higher values only when needed. The response includes truncated:true and totalCount when limited.

filterParameterName.description: Name of the parameter to filter on (e.g. 'Level', 'Area', 'Comments').

filterValue.description: Value to compare against. For numeric operators supply the number as a string.

filterOperator.description: Comparison operator for the filter. 'greater_than' and 'less_than' perform numeric comparison on the raw parameter value.

name: export_families
description: Export loaded families as .rfa files to a folder.

name: export_room_data
description:
Export room data from the current Revit project. Returns detailed information about each room including name, number, level, area, volume, perimeter, department, and more. Useful for generating room schedules, space analysis, and facility management data.

OPTIMIZATION:
- Use 'maxResults' to limit rooms returned (default 100). Response includes truncated flag and totalCount.

GUIDANCE:
- Get all rooms: returns name, number, area, volume, level, department, boundaries
- Use room data for space analysis, area calculations, or BIM validation
- Room boundaries can be used with create_floor to auto-generate floors

TIPS:
- Only returns placed rooms with valid area > 0
- Area and volume are in the project's display units
- Combine with create_views_from_rooms to auto-create room views
- Use get_elements_in_spatial_volume to find what's inside each room

includeUnplacedRooms.description: Whether to include unplaced rooms (rooms not yet placed in the model). Defaults to false.

includeNotEnclosedRooms.description: Whether to include rooms that are not fully enclosed. Defaults to false.

maxResults.description: Maximum rooms to return. Default 100. Response includes truncated:true and totalCount when limited.

name: export_schedule
description: Export a schedule view to CSV/TSV/TXT.

scheduleId.description: The element ID of the schedule to export

exportPath.description: File path for the exported schedule.

delimiter.description: Field delimiter for the exported file (default: Tab)

includeHeaders.description: Whether to include column headers in the export (default: true)

name: export_shared_parameter_file
description: Export the shared parameter file contents as structured data.

name: export_to_excel
description:
Export elements by category to an Excel (.xlsx) file with color-coded columns.
Column colors: green=instance parameter, yellow=type parameter, red=read-only.
File is saved to the specified path or Desktop by default.

GUIDANCE:
- "Export all doors to Excel": categories=["Doors"]
- "Export walls with Mark, Width, Height to Excel": categories=["Walls"], parameterNames=["Mark","Width","Height"]
- "Export everything including type parameters": includeTypeParameters=true
- "Save to specific path": filePath="C:/Exports/doors.xlsx"

categories.description: Category names to export (e.g. 'Walls', 'Doors'). Empty = all categories.

parameterNames.description: Parameter names to include. Empty = all discovered parameters.

includeTypeParameters.description: Include type-level parameters (shown in yellow columns).

colorCodeColumns.description: Color-code header cells: green=instance, yellow=type, red=read-only.

name: filter_by_parameter_value
description: Filter and select elements by parameter value conditions.

value.description: Value to compare against. Not required for is_empty/is_not_empty

returnParameters.description: Additional parameters to return for each matched element

name: find_undimensioned_elements
description: Find elements in the active view (or a specified view) that are not referenced by any dimension. Useful for QA/QC audits to ensure all structural and architectural elements are properly dimensioned.

categories.description: List of Revit built-in category names to check (e.g., 'OST_Walls', 'OST_Grids', 'OST_Columns'). Defaults to common structural/architectural categories.

viewId.description: View ID to check. Defaults to the active view.

limit.description: Maximum number of undimensioned elements to return (default: 500)

name: find_untagged_elements
description: Find elements in the active view (or a specified view) that do not have any tags. Useful for QA/QC audits to ensure all required elements are properly tagged before issuing drawings.

categories.description: List of Revit built-in category names to check (e.g., 'OST_Walls', 'OST_Doors', 'OST_Rooms'). Defaults to common architectural categories.

viewId.description: View ID to check. Defaults to the active view.

limit.description: Maximum number of untagged elements to return (default: 500)

name: generate_type_schedules
description: Abaco: generate a type-summary schedule for each given category (default walls, floors, ceilings), listing every unique type actually used with its key parameters and an instance count. Language-safe: works on Italian or English Revit. One call produces one schedule per category.

categories.description: BuiltInCategory names to schedule. Default: OST_Walls, OST_Floors, OST_Ceilings.

namePrefix.description: Prefix for each schedule name, e.g. 'Abaco - Muri'.

itemized.description: If false (default), rows collapse so each unique type appears once. If true, every instance is a row.

name: get_available_family_types
description:
Get available family types in the current Revit project. You can filter by category and family name, and limit the number of returned types.

GUIDANCE:
- Find wall types: categoryFilter="Walls" — returns all available wall families and types
- Find door types: categoryFilter="Doors" — needed before create_point_based_element
- Search all families: omit filter to see every loaded family type

TIPS:
- Always call this before creating elements to get exact family/type names
- Use load_family to load additional families from .rfa files
- Family names are case-sensitive — copy exact names from results

categoryList.description: List of Revit category names to filter by (e.g., 'OST_Walls', 'OST_Doors', 'OST_Furniture')

familyNameFilter.description: Filter family types by family name (partial match)

limit.description: Maximum number of family types to return

compact.description: Return summary counts only, without full data arrays. Saves tokens for large results.

name: get_compound_structure
description: Get the compound structure (layer stratigraphy) of a system family type (wall, floor, roof, ceiling). Returns all layers with thickness, material, and function.

typeId.description: Element ID of the type. Provide this OR typeName + category.

typeName.description: Name of the type (e.g. 'Generic - 200mm'). Use with category.

category.description: Category of the type. Required when using typeName.

name: get_current_view_elements
description:
Get elements from the current active view in Revit. You can filter by model categories (like Walls, Floors) or annotation categories (like Dimensions, Text). Use includeHidden to show/hide invisible elements and limit to control the number of returned elements.

GUIDANCE:
- See what's in the active view: call with no parameters
- Filter by category: use categoryFilter to narrow results (e.g. "Walls", "Doors")
- Use before operate_element to find elements to select/hide/isolate

TIPS:
- Returns element IDs, names, categories — use these IDs with other tools
- Large views may return many elements — use categoryFilter to limit results
- Combine with get_element_parameters to inspect specific elements

modelCategoryList.description: List of Revit model category names (e.g., 'OST_Walls', 'OST_Doors', 'OST_Floors')

annotationCategoryList.description: List of Revit annotation category names (e.g., 'OST_Dimensions', 'OST_WallTags', 'OST_TextNotes')

includeHidden.description: Whether to include hidden elements in the results

limit.description: Maximum number of elements to return

name: get_current_view_info
description: Get details about the active view (type, scale, crop, etc.).

name: get_element_parameters
description:
Get all parameters (instance and type) of one or more Revit elements by their IDs. Returns parameter names, values, storage types, and whether they are read-only or shared.

GUIDANCE:
- Inspect single element: provide elementId to see all instance + type parameters
- Discover parameter names: useful before export_elements_data or set_element_parameters
- Check current values before modifying with set_element_parameters

TIPS:
- Returns both instance and type parameters with current values
- Parameter names from this tool can be used in set_element_parameters, export_elements_data, create_schedule
- Read-only parameters are marked — these cannot be modified

elementIds.description: Array of Revit element IDs to get parameters for

includeTypeParameters.description: Include type parameters in addition to instance parameters (default: true)

compact.description: Return summary counts only, without full data arrays. Saves tokens for large results.

name: get_elements_by_workset
description: Get all elements belonging to a specific workset, with category summary and optional filtering.

worksetName.description: Name of the workset to query

categoryFilter.description: Optional list of category names to filter (e.g. ['Walls', 'Doors']). If omitted, returns all categories.

maxElements.description: Maximum number of element details to return. Default: 500. Category summary always includes all elements.

name: get_elements_in_spatial_volume
description:
Find all elements contained within spatial volumes: rooms, areas, or a custom bounding box. Returns elements grouped by volume with category, family, and type info. Use categoryFilter to narrow results.

GUIDANCE:
- Elements in a room: volumeType="room", volumeIds=[roomId]
- Elements in area: volumeType="area", volumeIds=[areaId]
- Custom bounding box: volumeType="custom", provide min/max XYZ in mm
- Filter by category: categoryFilter=["Furniture","Doors"] to limit results

TIPS:
- Great for room-based quantity takeoffs
- Use export_room_data to get room IDs first
- CategoryFilter narrows results to specific element types
- Combine with export_elements_data for detailed parameter extraction

volumeIds.description: IDs of rooms/areas to search within. If omitted, searches all rooms/areas.

volumeType.description: Type of volume: room, area, or custom bounding box. Default: room.

customMinX.description: Min X in mm (for custom bounding box).

customMinY.description: Min Y in mm (for custom bounding box).

customMinZ.description: Min Z in mm (for custom bounding box).

customMaxX.description: Max X in mm (for custom bounding box).

customMaxY.description: Max Y in mm (for custom bounding box).

customMaxZ.description: Max Z in mm (for custom bounding box).

maxElementsPerVolume.description: Max elements returned per room/area/volume. Default 100.

name: get_linked_elements
description: Query elements from linked Revit models by category.

linkName.description: Filter by link name (partial match). Empty = all linked models.

categories.description: Category names to query. Empty = all categories.

parameterNames.description: Parameters to extract per element. Empty = just ID/Category/Name.

name: get_material_properties
description: Get detailed physical/thermal properties of a material.

materialId.description: The Revit element ID of the material

materialName.description: Material name (case-insensitive). Used if materialId not provided.

name: get_material_quantities
description:
Calculate material quantities and takeoffs from the current Revit project. Returns detailed information about each material including name, class, area, volume, and element counts. Useful for cost estimation, material ordering, and sustainability analysis.

GUIDANCE:
- Material takeoff: returns areas and volumes per material across selected categories
- Cost estimation: combine quantities with material costs
- Compare alternatives: run for different design options

TIPS:
- Filter by category for focused takeoffs (e.g. just walls or floors)
- Values are in project display units (m², m³ or ft², ft³)
- Use get_materials and get_material_properties for material details

categoryFilters.description: Optional list of Revit category names to filter by (e.g., ['OST_Walls', 'OST_Floors', 'OST_Roofs']). If not specified, all categories are included.

selectedElementsOnly.description: Whether to only analyze currently selected elements. Defaults to false (analyze entire project).

name: get_materials
description: List materials in the project, optionally filtered by class.

materialClass.description: Filter materials by class (case-insensitive exact match, e.

nameFilter.description: Filter materials whose name contains this substring (case-insensitive)

name: get_phases
description: List all phases in the project with their sequence order.

includePhaseFilters.description: Include phase filters in addition to phases (default: true)

name: get_project_info
description:
Get comprehensive project information from the active Revit document, including project metadata, phases, worksets, Revit links, and levels.

GUIDANCE:
- Project overview: returns project name, address, levels, phases, worksets, links
- Check levels before creating views or placing elements
- Verify worksets and phases for proper element organization

TIPS:
- Call this first in a new project to understand structure
- Level names are needed for create_view, create_level, and element placement
- Phase information is important for renovation/phasing workflows

compact.description: Return summary counts only, without full data arrays. Saves tokens for large results.

name: get_room_openings
description: Find doors and/or windows in rooms with full parameter extraction. Fast phase-aware lookup via FromRoom/ToRoom.

roomIds.description: Room ElementIds to query. Omit for all rooms.

roomNumbers.description: Room numbers to query (alternative to roomIds). Omit for all rooms.

levelName.description: Filter rooms by level name (partial match, e.g. 'L2'). Combinable with roomNumbers.

elementType.description: Type of openings to find: doors, windows, or both.

parameterNames.description: Specific parameter names to extract (empty = all key parameters).

name: get_schedule_data
description: Read data from an existing schedule view.

scheduleId.description: Schedule element ID. If omitted or 0, lists all schedules in the project.

name: get_selected_elements
description: Get info about currently selected elements in Revit.

limit.description: Maximum number of elements to return

name: get_shared_parameters
description: List shared parameters and their category bindings.

name: get_warnings
description:
Get all warnings/errors in the current Revit model. Warnings indicate issues like duplicate elements, overlapping geometry, room separation problems, etc. Useful for model health auditing and quality control.

GUIDANCE:
- Model health check: returns all Revit warnings/errors
- Quality audit: identify duplicate elements, overlapping geometry, etc.
- Pre-submission review: fix warnings before model delivery

TIPS:
- Warnings don't prevent work but indicate potential issues
- Common warnings: duplicate instances, room not enclosed, overlapping walls
- Use check_model_health for a comprehensive scored audit
- Fix high-severity warnings first

maxWarnings.description: Maximum number of warnings to return (default: 500)

categoryFilter.description: Filter warnings containing this text in the description (case-insensitive)

name: get_worksets
description: List worksets with element counts and open/close status.

name: import_from_excel
description: Import data from Excel into Revit element parameters.

filePath.description: Full path to the .xlsx file to import.

dryRun.description: Preview changes without modifying the model. Always try this first.

name: import_table
description: Import a table (CSV/JSON) and apply values to element parameters.

filePath.description: Path to CSV or TSV file on disk

viewName.description: Name for the created view. Auto-generated if omitted

name: lines_per_view_count
description: Count detail lines and model lines per view across the project. Helps identify views with excessive line work that may impact performance. Results sorted by total line count descending.

threshold.description: Only return views with total lines >= this threshold (default: 0, returns all views)

includeDetailLines.description: Include detail lines in the count (default: true)

includeModelLines.description: Include model lines in the count (default: true)

limit.description: Maximum number of views to return (default: 200)

name: list_family_sizes
description: List all families in the project with their instance count, type count, and metadata. Helps identify bloated families (too many types), unused families (zero instances), and in-place families that may impact performance.

limit.description: Maximum number of families to return (default: 50)

categories.description: Filter by category names (e.g., 'Doors', 'Windows', 'OST_Doors'). If omitted, returns all categories.

name: list_schedulable_fields
description: List all available schedule fields for a given Revit category. Use before creating schedules to discover valid field names.

scheduleType.description: Schedule type to query fields for. Default: regular

name: load_family
description: Load a .rfa family, list loaded families, or duplicate a type.

familyPath.description: Full path to .rfa file (required for 'load' action)

categoryFilter.description: Filter families by category name (for 'list' action, case-insensitive)

sourceTypeId.description: Source family type ID to duplicate (for 'duplicate_type' action)

newTypeName.description: Name for the new duplicated type (for 'duplicate_type' action)

name: load_selection
description: Load a previously saved element selection by name.

name.description: Name of the saved selection to load. If omitted, lists all saved selections

selectInView.description: If true, select the elements in the current Revit view

name: manage_links
description: List, reload, unload, or remove linked Revit/CAD/IFC files.

action.description: Action: 'list' shows all links, 'reload' reloads a link, 'unload' unloads a link

linkId.description: Link element ID (required for reload/unload actions)

name: manage_project_parameters
description: Add, list, or delete project parameters with category bindings.

action.description: list, create, delete, or modify category bindings.

dataType.description: Parameter data type. Only used for action="create". Default: "Text".

isShared.description: Reserved for future use. Default: false.

name: manage_unplaced_views
description: List or delete views not placed on any sheet.

viewTypes.description: Filter by view types. If omitted, all view types are included.

filterName.description: Only include views whose name contains this text (case-insensitive).

excludeNames.description: Exclude views whose name contains any of these strings.

dryRun.description: Preview only, no actual delete. Default: true.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of views to return in the response.

name: manage_view_templates
description: List, create, apply, or modify view templates.

newName.description: New name for duplicate or rename operations.

filterViewType.description: Filter by view type when listing (e.g. 'FloorPlan', 'Section', 'ThreeD').

name: match_element_properties
description: Copy parameter values from a source element to target elements.

sourceElementId.description: Source element ID to copy parameter values from

targetElementIds.description: Target element IDs to copy parameter values to

parameterNames.description: Parameter names to copy. Default: all writable params.

includeTypeParameters.description: Also copy type parameters (default: false, instance only)

name: measure_between_elements
description: Measure distance between two elements or points.

point1.description: First point in mm (alternative to elementId1)

point2.description: Second point in mm (alternative to elementId2)

name: modify_element
description: Move, rotate, or mirror elements in the model.

elementIds.description: Array of element IDs to modify

translation.description: Translation vector in mm (required for 'move' action).

rotationCenter.description: Center point of rotation in mm (required for 'rotate' action)

rotationAngle.description: Rotation angle in degrees (required for 'rotate' action)

mirrorPlaneOrigin.description: Origin point of the mirror plane in mm (required for 'mirror' action)

mirrorPlaneNormal.description: Normal vector of the mirror plane (required for 'mirror' action).

copyOffset.description: Offset vector for copy in mm (required for 'copy' action)

name: modify_schedule
description: Modify an existing Revit schedule: add/remove fields, set/clear filters, set/clear sorting, rename, or change display options.

scheduleId.description: Schedule ElementId. Provide either scheduleId or scheduleName.

action.description: Action to perform on the schedule.

fieldNames.description: Field/parameter names for add_field or remove_field actions.

filterType.description: Equal, NotEqual, GreaterThan, LessThan, Contains, etc.

filters.description: Filters to apply for set_filters action.

isItemized.description: Show every instance as a separate row (for set_display_options).

name: navigate_view
description: Navigate views: activate a view, zoom to fit, zoom to specific elements, zoom in/out, or close a view.

viewName.description: Target view name (partial match). Alternative to viewId.

zoomFactor.description: Zoom factor for 'zoom' action (>1 = in, <1 = out).

name: operate_element
description: Perform operations: select, color, hide, unhide, pin, unpin, etc.

items.description: A valid Revit element ID to operate on

elementIds.description: Array of Revit element IDs to perform the specified action on

action.description: The operation to perform on elements.

colorValue.description: RGB color values for SetColor action. Default is red [255,0,0].

data.description: Parameters for operating on Revit elements with specific actions

name: override_graphics
description: Override element graphics (color, lineweight, pattern, transparency).

action.description: set overrides or reset to default (default: set)

name: place_viewport
description: Place a view on a sheet at specified coordinates.

sheetId.description: ID of the sheet to place the viewport on

viewId.description: ID of the view to place

positionX.description: X position on the sheet in mm (from sheet origin)

positionY.description: Y position on the sheet in mm (from sheet origin)

name: purge_unused
description: Find and remove unused families, types, and materials.

dryRun.description: If true (default), only reports what would be purged without deleting.

maxElements.description: Maximum number of elements to purge in one operation (default: 500)

name: query_stored_data
description: Query data previously stored via store_project_data.

name: rename_families
description: Rename loaded families with find/replace or prefix/suffix.

operation.description: Type of rename operation to perform

renameTypes.description: If true, also rename family type (FamilySymbol) names

dryRun.description: If true, only previews changes without applying. Set to false to apply.

name: rename_views
description: Rename views using find/replace, prefix, suffix, or regex.

prefix.description: Prefix to add to view names (required for prefix operation)

suffix.description: Suffix to add to view names (required for suffix operation)

findText.description: Text to find in view names (required for find_replace operation)

viewTypes.description: Filter by view types. If omitted, all view types are included.

filterName.description: Only rename views whose name contains this text

dryRun.description: If true (default), only previews changes without applying.

name: rename_workset
description: Rename an existing user workset.

currentName.description: Current name of the workset to rename

newName.description: New name for the workset. Cannot contain { } [ ] | ; characters.

name: renumber_elements
description: Renumber rooms, doors, windows, or parking by location or name.

elementIds.description: Specific element IDs to renumber. If omitted, uses targetCategory.

parameterName.description: Parameter to set number on. Default: built-in number param.

dryRun.description: If true (default), only preview changes. Set to false to apply.

name: save_selection
description: Save the current element selection with a name for later recall.

elementIds.description: Specific element IDs to save. If omitted, saves current Revit selection

overwrite.description: If true, overwrite an existing selection with the same name

name: say_hello
description: Display a greeting dialog in Revit. Tests MCP connection.

message.description: Optional custom message to display in the dialog. Defaults to 'Hello MCP!'

name: section_box_from_selection
description: Set or create a 3D section box fitted around selected elements.

elementIds.description: Element IDs to fit the section box around.

useCurrentSelection.description: If true, uses currently selected elements in Revit.

offsetMm.description: Offset buffer in mm around the bounding box of elements. Default: 1000mm.

duplicateView.description: If true, creates a new 3D view.

viewName.description: Name for the new 3D view (only if duplicateView=true).

isolateElements.description: If true, temporarily isolates elements in the view. Default: false.

name: send_code_to_revit
description: Execute custom C# code in Revit. Your code runs inside a static method with signature: `public static object Execute(Document document, object[] parameters)`. Available variables: `document` (Autodesk.Revit.DB.Document — the active document), `parameters` (object[] — optional args). Auto-imported namespaces: System, System.Linq, Autodesk.Revit.DB, Autodesk.Revit.UI, System.Collections.Generic. Use `return` to send results back. In 'auto' mode your code runs inside a Transaction; use 'none' to manage transactions yourself.

code.description: C# code to execute. Use `document` to access the active Revit Document. Use `return` to send results back. Example: `var walls = new FilteredElementCollector(document).OfClass(typeof(Wall)).GetElementCount(); return "Walls: " + walls;`

parameters.description: Optional execution parameters that will be passed to your code

transactionMode.description: Transaction mode: 'auto' (default) wraps code in a Revit Transaction; 'none' lets the code manage its own transactions

name: set_active_workset
description: Set the active workset. New elements will be created in this workset.

worksetName.description: Name of the workset to set as active

name: set_compound_structure
description: Set or replace the compound structure (layer stratigraphy) of a system family type. Optionally duplicate the type first to avoid modifying all instances.

typeId.description: Element ID of the type. Provide this OR typeName + category.

typeName.description: Name of the type to modify. Use with category.

category.description: Category of the type. Required when using typeName.

duplicateAsName.description: If provided, duplicate the type with this name before modifying. Prevents changing all existing instances.

widthMm.description: Layer thickness in millimeters. Must be 0 for Membrane layers.

materialName.description: Material name to assign. If omitted and materialId not set, uses 'By Category'.

materialId.description: Material element ID. Alternative to materialName.

wraps.description: Whether this layer participates in wrapping at inserts/ends. Default: false.

layers.description: Array of layers from exterior to interior (walls) or top to bottom (floors/roofs/ceilings).

name: set_element_parameters
description: Set a parameter value on one or more elements.

parameterName.description: Name of the parameter to set

name: set_element_phase
description: Assign created/demolished phase to elements.

createdPhaseId.description: Phase ID to set as the created phase for the element

demolishedPhaseId.description: Phase ID to set as the demolished phase for the element

name: set_element_workset
description: Move elements to a different workset.

worksetName.description: Name of the target workset to move the element to

name: set_material_appearance
description: Modify the visual appearance of a material: shading color, transparency, shininess, smoothness, pattern colors, and rendering properties (diffuse color, glossiness).

materialId.description: Material element ID. Provide this OR materialName.

materialName.description: Material name (case-insensitive). Use with materialId as alternative.

useRenderAppearanceForShading.description: Use render appearance for realistic shading

name: set_material_assets
description: Modify the structural and/or thermal asset properties of a material. Values are in Revit internal units (metric: kg, m, Pa, W, etc.).

materialId.description: Material element ID. Provide this OR materialName.

youngModulus.description: Young's modulus (Pa). Sets X, Y, Z to the same value.

poissonRatio.description: Poisson's ratio (dimensionless). Sets X, Y, Z to the same value.

shearModulus.description: Shear modulus (Pa). Sets X, Y, Z to the same value.

thermalExpansionCoefficient.description: Thermal expansion coefficient (1/°C). Sets X, Y, Z to the same value.

thermalDensity.description: Thermal density (kg/m³). Separate from structural density.

name: set_material_properties
description: Set identity and product information on Revit materials (Comments, Description, Manufacturer, Model, URL, Cost, Mark, Keynote, Name). Preview with dryRun=true.

requests.description: Array of materials to update with their new property values

dryRun.description: If true (default), preview changes without applying. Set to false to execute.

name: set_view_crop
description: Control the crop box of a view: enable/disable, set bounds from elements with offset or explicit coordinates, show/hide crop boundary.

cropActive.description: Enable (true) or disable (false) the crop region.

cropVisible.description: Show (true) or hide (false) the crop boundary lines.

elementIds.description: Fit crop box to these elements with offset.

minXMm.description: Explicit crop min X in mm.

minYMm.description: Explicit crop min Y in mm.

maxXMm.description: Explicit crop max X in mm.

maxYMm.description: Explicit crop max Y in mm.

name: store_project_data
description: Store key-value data in the Revit project as extensible storage.

project_name.description: The name of the Revit project

project_status.description: Project status (e.g., Active, Completed, On Hold)

metadata.description: Additional project metadata as key-value pairs

name: store_room_data
description: Store custom key-value data on room elements.

project_name.description: The name of the Revit project this room belongs to

room_id.description: Unique identifier for the room (Revit Element ID)

metadata.description: Additional room metadata as key-value pairs

rooms.description: Array of room data to store

name: sync_csv_parameters
description: Sync element parameters with a CSV file (import/export/roundtrip).

parameters.description: Parameter name → value pairs to set

dryRun.description: If true (default), only preview changes without applying. Set to false to apply.

name: tag_all_rooms
description: Tag rooms in the current view with room tags.

useLeader.description: Whether to use a leader line when creating the tags

tagTypeId.description: The ID of the specific room tag family type to use.

roomIds.description: Optional array of specific room element IDs to tag.

name: tag_all_walls
description: Tag walls in the current view with wall tags.

useLeader.description: Whether to use a leader line when creating the tags

tagTypeId.description: The ID of the specific wall tag family type to use.

name: transfer_parameters
description: Copy parameter values between elements of different categories.

sourceElementId.description: ID of the source element to copy parameters from.

targetElementIds.description: IDs of target elements to copy parameters to.

includeType.description: Also transfer type parameter values. Default: false.

dryRun.description: Preview changes without applying. Default: true. Set to false to execute.

name: wipe_empty_tags
description: Find and remove tags that have empty text or reference deleted/invalid elements. DESTRUCTIVE: defaults to dryRun=true (preview only). Set dryRun=false to actually delete empty tags.

dryRun.description: If true (default), only reports empty tags without deleting them. Set to false to actually delete.

viewId.description: Limit scope to a specific view. If omitted, scans the entire document.

categories.description: Filter by tag category names (e.g., 'Wall Tags', 'Door Tags'). If omitted, checks all tag categories.

name: workflow_clash_review
description: Detect clashes between two categories and visualize results.

categoryB.description: Second Revit category name to check (e.g. 'Ducts', 'Mechanical Equipment').

createSectionBox.description: Create section box around clashes. Default: true.

name: workflow_data_roundtrip
description: Export parameters to CSV, edit externally, and re-import.

categories.description: Category names to export (e.g. 'Walls', 'Doors'). Empty = all categories.

parameterNames.description: Parameter names to include. Empty = all discovered parameters.

includeTypeParameters.description: Include type-level parameters (shown in yellow columns).

name: workflow_model_audit
description: Run a comprehensive model health audit with scoring.

includeWarnings.description: Include warning details in the report.

includeFamilies.description: Include unused family type detection (slower on large models).

maxWarnings.description: Maximum warnings to include in detail.

name: workflow_room_documentation
description: Auto-generate room documentation: plans, sections, schedules.

levelName.description: Filter rooms by level name. If omitted, all rooms in the model are included.

createSections.description: Create callout section views from each room's bounding box. Default true.

offset.description: Section view boundary offset in mm. Default: 300.

name: workflow_sheet_set
description: Auto-create sheets from views with title blocks and viewports.

sheets.description: Array of sheet definitions with number and name.

titleBlockName.description: Title block name. Default: first available.
